item.description: League sources say that the Heat remain in full-speed pursuit of Antetokounmpo but also indicate that the Boston Celtics have not yet abandoned hope of completing a trade for The Greek Freak. They are the two teams, sources say, that have engaged in the most advanced trade talks to date with the Bucks.

item.title: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the …
item.description: Heat Central: Chad Johnson is doubling down on his sources saying Giannis is coming to Miami: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the months. The sources that I do have, they know what they’re talking about. Which is why I’ve came on here and say things that I’ve said. All the other stuff, with Boston, and him going anywhere, none of that is going to happen. That’s just created dialogue to keep people guessing on where he might go.”

item.title: Bucks interested in Hugo Gonzalez
item.description: The Celtics, like Miami, would naturally be asked to add players and future draft compensation to the ultimate trade construction. Sources say that the Bucks have interest in Boston's prized young Spanish guard Hugo Gonzalez.

item.title: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. Dallas is telling teams Kyrie Irving is not available. The Pistons have expressed interest in Trey Murphy, but so have a zillion other teams, and New Orleans' threshold for trading Trey Murphy away is really, really high. So the Pistons have looked at other targets. Tyler Herro, if he ends up going to Milwaukee in a Giannis trade, we reported that Thursday night, we revealed that, that the Pistons have interest in Tyler Herro, but

item.title: Shams: If Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee
item.description: Heat Central: “All I’ll say to you Greeny, if the Milwaukee Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee” — @ShamsCharania says Jaylen Brown will be staying in MIL after a Giannis trade

item.title: “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo WILL BE traded before the draft and is focused on Boston or Miami, per @ShamsCharania “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, it’s going to be a one for one deal. It’s going to consist of MIL and one of these two teams. There’s no 3rd and 4th teams.”

item.title: “I want to tell you that process is already underway. …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have ALREADY been trying to identify players to add after a Giannis trade, per @WindhorstESPN “I want to tell you that process is already underway. They have already identified players that they can chase if they get this deal done.”

item.title: Giannis is content with either of these two …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Per Shams: Giannis is expected to be traded before tomorrow’s draft Likely headed to either Boston (who have made Jaylen Brown available) or Miami Giannis is content with either of these two destinations (willing to sign extension)

item.title: Jaylen Brown to be traded regardless of whether Celtics land Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst believes Jaylen Brown will be traded even if the Celtics miss on Giannis and is accepting that reality: “I believe if the Bucks choose the Miami Heat offer, Jaylen Brown will get traded somewhere else. They are going to move on here…. From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizing it’s a chance to turn the page and may be getting his own team”

item.title: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I asked Giannis associate why not say Miami and only Miami. Answer was: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be OK with Boston too. It was conveyed to at least one (far) West team that he would not extend there, which dissuaded that team. But Giannis camp ultimately was not going to try to dissuade every team.

item.title: Pistons in trade talks regarding Tyler Herro
item.description: The Free Press has confirmed with multiple people within the league not permitted to speak publicly that the Pistons have been involved in discussions regarding Tyler Herro. And though it remains to be seen if the deal will get across the finish line, three of their key role players – Isaiah Stewart, Duncan Robinson and Ron Holland – are candidates to be moved in such a trade.

item.title: Pistons' assets include all of their first-round …
item.description: As the adage goes, it takes money to make money. The Pistons' assets include all of their first-round picks, including No. 21 in Tuesday's draft, a trove of second-round picks and several good players on contracts that can be easily moved.

item.title: Celtics not willing to add multiple firsts for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Bucks owners are “split” on which decision to make with a Giannis trade and Boston is NOT willing to add multiple 1st round picks like Miami, per @JakeLFischer “We’re being told there is some type of split faction in MIL right now among the major stakeholders for the Bucks… the Celtics to our understanding, are not willing to offer the same level of multiple years of draft capital that the Miami Heat are” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis is on his way back from the states and ready for a trade to Miami or Boston, per @ShamsCharania "At this very hour the MIL Bucks are moving closer and closer to a trade resolution for Giannis.... He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of right now. He's ready for a trade whether it's Miami or Boston"

item.title: The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Bucks, as we reported last night, have certainly asked for Hugo. The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep him. Not exactly a make-or-break element but one of the variables that factors into this Boston vs. Miami struggle.

item.title: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites to acquire Giannis tonight or tomorrow

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade may expand before July 6
item.description: The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand. Last June, the Phoenix Suns and Houston Rockets agreed to a deal that ultimately increased to a historic seven-team trade.

item.title: The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at …
item.description: The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: the Heat and Boston Celtics, who were both on Antetokounmpo's list of preferred destinations. The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline in February, and aggressively pursued him this time around, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, according to sources.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo's camp told Bucks he would not sign a long-term extension with the team
item.description: Sources told ESPN that Antetokounmpo and his agent, Alex Saratsis, consistently informed the Bucks from May 2025 through May 2026 that the franchise legend wanted a trade from the organization because he believed it was best for both sides to part ways. Antetokounmpo's reps informed the Bucks several times over the past 13 months -- including before, during and after the season -- that he would not sign another long-term extension with the team, according to sources. The Bucks made the decision

item.title: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's Hugo Gonzalez hang-up. Had been hearing all day the Heat did not want to part with the Illinois product on top of their Herro-Ware-Jacquez-No. 13 structure. That's an All-Star guard, three recent first-round picks still on rookie deals, a second lottery pick in tomorrow's loaded draft class, plus two more future firsts and a swap... It's really a sizable haul for Giannis Antetokounmpo. Compared to Jaylen Brown and two first-round

item.title: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks that they're getting Giannis. Cautious optimism pays off lol. Miami is exactly where Giannis preferred to be, someone who works for him reiterated again today.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on January 6 to sign an extension for: 4 years/$275M if he opts-out 3 years/$214M if he opts-in The opt-in is slightly more lucrative

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo heads to Miami in six player deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BLOCKBUSTER: The Milwaukee Bucks are trading franchise icon Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis to the Miami Heat for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, 3 first-round picks (including No. 13), 1 pick swap and 1 second-rounder, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected first-rounders in 2031 and 2033 along with No. 13, a pick swap in 2030 and a 2033 second-rounder, according to sources. The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand.

item.title: Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: Bucks didn't want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee?
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Sources say Bucks owner Jimmy Haslem was a driving force in taking the Heat deal for Giannis Antetokounmpo over Boston’s offer. Haslem didn’t want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee in a year after dealing with Giannis and Myles Garrett trade demands. Haslem wanted certainty, and the Miami deal provided more of that with more total assets in return to Milwaukee.

item.title: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade. They could trade their 2029 first-round pick on the condition that their 2027 first-round pick (top-14 protected) conveys to Charlotte. They could try to regain control of it in a trade with the Hornets.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with Heat, Celtics and Timberwolves before trade
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The 3 teams Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with: Miami, Boston and Minnesota. The Heat land a foundational piece and now have the attention of top players searching for a new home.

item.title: They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now. They'll need to reduce their payroll to create space to re-sign him. They could also use more flexibility to open up the full $15M mid-level.

item.title: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign …
item.description: Barry Jackson: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign multiyear deal with Miami instead - and if Norman Powell re-signs - that's a rotation of Giannis, Bam, Mitchell, Powell, Wiggins, with Larsson, Bobby Portis and player/players acquired through 3 different exceptions. Pretty, pretty good.

item.title: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s. The 2030 swap with the Heat could potentially offset the swap they already owe to the Trail Blazers. They could really use more second-round picks.

item.title: With NBA and NHL teams now laser-focused on offseason …
item.description: With NBA and NHL teams now laser-focused on offseason business, Scripps Sports — with local rights to five hockey teams and one basketball team — is in acquisition mode. Scripps already locked up the Pistons last month and was in the running for the Heat. “We have a business model that’s working really well for us ... in markets that we’re already doing business where we have typically an NBC, ABC, CBS or Fox station,” Scripps Sports President Brian Lawlor said. “We’re in 40-something markets. T

item.title: Landing Giannis may force Heat to part ways with Norman Powell
item.description: Bobby Marks: I think it potentially could cost you Norman Powell, your All-Star from last year, who is an unrestricted free agent. Remember, if we deal with these apron rules, right? Miami now becomes hard-capped at the first apron. They're limited as far as what they can go out and offer Norman Powell and the deal. And when you look at their back court, their back court is a little bit depleted here. They need a little bit more front court help. And they've got about five roster spots to go out

item.title: Teams preparing for 'bidding war' for Jaylen Brown after Celtics miss on Giannis
item.description: Heat Central: Teams are now preparing to send offers for Jaylen Brown after Celtics strikes out on Giannis, Per @WindhorstESPN “What I expect to happen, is a bidding war for Jaylen Brown. In the most recent days, teams have been preparing for this eventuality… I think it’ll take time to play out, but if there is a draft pick involved, it could happen today” We got Giannis and Blew up Boston all in one

item.title: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard …
item.description: Get Up: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard conversations moving forward about the fallout from a trade that didn't happen. ... You've gotta get a deal done if you're the Celtics." — @ShamsCharania on what the Giannis trade to Miami means for Jaylen Brown

item.title: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say …
item.description: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say it remains unclear if Tyler Herro will remain with the team. While there is interest in making the Milwaukee native part of the his hometown’s program, sources say the Bucks will also listen to offers for the 26-year-old former All-Star. Herro is believed to have a robust market, with the Detroit Pistons known to be among the many teams interested in him. Since this trade won’t be finalized until July 6, it remains possible for additional te

item.title: Windhorst says Norman Powell will be GONE, unless he …
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst says Norman Powell will be GONE, unless he takes a massive pay cut: “They’re gonna lose their All Star, Norman Powell. Unless he takes a crazy discount. He’s gonna be gone… Andy Ellisburg their GM, is superior at his job. Erik Spoelstra, their developmental staff, I’m betting on the Heat. Just not in 2026-2027”

item.title: Heat attempted to include Davion Mitchell in Giannis trade
item.description: Per league sources, the Heat attempted to include point guard Davion Mitchell in the deal instead of Jakucionis. However, because Mitchell is entering the final year of his deal, as are Herro and Jaquez Jr., the Bucks made it clear that they had no interest in three of the four players in the deal having one season remaining. So the Bucks pushed for, and landed, Jakucionis. The 20-year-old, 6-foot-5 guard has three guaranteed seasons left on his rookie deal, including two team options.

item.title: “One guy that I already heard attached to them is …
item.description: Heat Central: Khris Middleton has already been linked to the Miami Heat, per @WindhorstESPN. “One guy that I already heard attached to them is Khris Middleton. Who obviously has a long history with Giannis and Bobby Portis… I don’t think you’re looking at the Heat instantaneously as title contenders”

item.title: Haynes: Tyler Herro thrilled about fresh start
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: Tyler Herro is thrilled about a fresh start and playing for his hometown team the Milwaukee Bucks. Herro always envisioned returning home at some point during his NBA career.

item.title: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron …
item.description: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron James with its full midlevel exception, but a James return is considered a long shot. The Heat also has available a $5.5 million biannual exception as well as a $5.6 million exception from the Haywood Highsmith trade with Brooklyn. That exception expires Aug. 17.

item.title: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough young players and assets to get a deal done." @ShamsCharania says Boston offered Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo before he was traded to Miami 👀

item.title: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: They're very happy that they got Bobby Portis in this trade. They're really excited about how he's gonna fit in with that lineup" - @ramonashelburne

item.title: Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any …
item.description: HoopsHype: New Bucks guard Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any Wisconsin-born player in NBA history. Now you know that.

item.title: Miami could look to trade Nikola Jovic
item.description: It has also been suggested that Miami could look now to move Nikola Jovic to try to acquire some shooting to help space the floor for Antetokounmpo and his new frontcourt sidekick Bam Adebayo.

item.title: So the Miami Heat need a slashing 2 guard 👀
item.description: Jaden Ivey: So the Miami Heat need a slashing 2 guard 👀

item.title: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract extension in Milwaukee and he wanted out.. The Bucks were open for business and they're moving forward after making this trade with the Miami Heat" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "In May of 2025 Giannis Antetokounmpo and his agent were very direct with the Bucks that it was time to move on.. He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt like they could no longer compete for a championship" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: Miami unlikely to be able to re-sign Norman Powell
item.description: Heat Central: We can probably take Norman Powell off this starting 5. The Heat are expecting him not to be there this offseason. Financially it’s going to be very difficult to keep him. They could participate in a sign a trade with Norman Powell…. They understand they have to fill this roster out. My understanding talking to people in Miami, shooting, shooting, and shooting.

item.title: If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect …
item.description: Will the Pistons make another run at Herro, who’s currently set to join his hometown Bucks? Or will Detroit attempt to pivot by making a run at Norman Powell in free agency or via trade for Trey Murphy III, whom they’ve expressed interest in? If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect Pelicans executive Troy Weaver to insist on reuniting with Stewart, one of his favorite players from his Pistons tenure.

item.title: NBA executives wondering if Norman Powell will leave Heat after Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: A few executives who spoke with HoopsHype believe Miami’s All-Star guard Norman Powell could now be a candidate to leave the Heat, and the Detroit Pistons are among the teams who’ll be trying to pry him from South Beach, league sources told HoopsHype. Andrew Wiggins has a $30.17 million player option for the upcoming season. Executives are waiting to see if Wiggins will exercise his option or decline it with the intention of signing a new multi-year deal with Miami, or if he’ll consider a new de

item.title: Bucks plan to keep Kel'el Ware for now
item.description: Jake Fischer: I do believe Milwaukee has tried to package Nos. 10 and 13 to move up in tonight's first round, but I don't believe there's going to be any trades in the top-10. Sources say the Bucks, at this point, plan to keep Kel'el Ware, despite interest from rival teams like the Lakers. Milwaukee coveted Ware and Kasparas Jakučionis in their recent drafts. The Bucks also value Tyler Herro, sources say, and don't necessarily plan to move him, while Milwaukee will field offers for the All-Star

item.title: Heat have the full allowable $7.9M to spend on such a …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Worth considering amid Heat's hard-cap crunch is that a second-round pick counts as about half of a standard minimum contract. Heat have the full allowable $7.9M to spend on such a pick or picks, money that does not count against cap, tax or apron. Heat already hold No. 41 pick.

item.title: Bucks, one day after trading Giannis Antetokounmpo to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bucks, one day after trading Giannis Antetokounmpo to Miami, will be selecting Brayden Burries with the No. 10 pick, sources say.

item.title: Comps I’ve heard for Morez Johnson Jr., the Michigan …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Comps I’ve heard for Morez Johnson Jr., the Michigan PF/C picked by the Mavs at No. 9: Bam Adebayo and Al Horford

item.title: Bucks take Nate Ament at No. 13
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will select Nate Ament with the No. 13 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. This pick was acquired from the Miami Heat in the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade.

item.title: But, we're expecting plenty of movement in the second …
item.description: ‪Jake Fischer: Halfway through the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft, we've yet to see a trade of picks outside of Miami sending No. 13 to Milwaukee for Giannis Antetokounmpo. But, we're expecting plenty of movement in the second half, particularly across picks Nos. 20-30.

item.title: “Giannis will never win a championship with him being …
item.description: NBACentral: “Giannis will never win a championship with him being a number 1 option. I repeat. Giannis Antetokounmpo will never win another championship with him being the number 1 option” - Kendrick Perkins (h/t @HeatCulture13)

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo reacted to Wes Edens' ultimatum with resignation: 'They're gonna trade me'
item.description: Memories of Game 5 of the 2021 NBA Finals, a win that put the Milwaukee Bucks one game from their championship, coursed through him like the intravenous fluids he needed that night four years earlier. But when a senior team official wandered into view, his mood immediately shifted. Antetokounmpo derisively referenced aloud, without prompt, a comment by Bucks co-owner Wes Edens a day earlier, saying the franchise legend would either sign a contract extension or be traded. Antetokounmpo felt it co

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of …
item.description: Then on the team’s media day on Sept. 30, on a Zoom call from Greece because he and the team said he contracted COVID-19, Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of Edens’ statement that they had the on-court “meeting” at the practice facility in early May. Edens was annoyed, but two high-level team sources said he did not carry a grudge. Rivers held another remote training camp in 2025, this time in Miami. Even with a roster that had been completely turned over from the one he took over i

item.title: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker …
item.description: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker room leaks, but his mental state was chronicled nearly all season by anonymous sources. He pleaded for accountability but then tried to pass off those reports as someone else’s doing. He would call his teammates selfish but then stand on a visiting team’s court with a former coach and yell about how that person would make sure he got the ball.

item.title: Detroit continues to express trade interest in Tyler Herro
item.description: Marc Stein: With Reaves now sure to stay in LA, as was long believed to be his clear preference, league sources say Detroit continues to express trade interest in Milwaukee's soon-to-be acquired Tyler Herro. The Bucks are expected to listen to offers but could also elect to keep Herro.

item.title: Buddy Hield a potential target for Miami
item.description: Also, keep an eye on Buddy Hield. Only $3M of his $9.6M salary is guaranteed. The Hawks must make a decision by Thursday, per his contract. Hield, who has had interest in Heat in the past, averaged 7.6 points and shot 34.9 in 51 games for Golden State and Atlanta.

item.title: "When I first came here, I had to sacrifice. I was the …
item.description: Shaquille O'Neal: "When I first came here, I had to sacrifice. I was the man everywhere I went. But I was telling them because I had some a question. I said because I don't remember playing against them. You know who he was. Because they all know I don't look at no tape, I don't pay attention. I just come, I just show up and play. So during the playoffs, I'm watching this motherfucker (Dwyane Wade) kill BD. I'm like, who's this motherfucker? And I know, I can see it because I seen him before. I

item.title: "(Miami Heat) are going to lose Norm Powell in free …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "(Miami Heat) are going to lose Norm Powell in free agency because of the trade and because they took a lot of more money back and some of the restrictions that come with it. So you basically have five roster spots to give to players on the veteran minimum exception. You need some shooting, you need a little bit more depth in your front court here. It is a matter of what players are willing to take a little bit of a discount to go down to South Florida."

item.title: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel …
item.description: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel that an earlier construct of the Heat’s proposal for Giannis Antetokounmpo included Davion Mitchell instead of Kasparas Jakucionis. The Bucks, however, insisted on the youthful promise and rookie-scale contract of Jakucionis.

item.title: Heat exercise team option on Pelle Larsson
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat announce they have exercised the 2026-27 team option on guard Pelle Larsson, which will make him a restricted free agent next summer unless extended in the interim.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo on getting back to Finals: It doesn't matter if I'm a role player, I want to get there again
item.description: “Some way, somehow, I have to get there again,” Antetokounmpo told the Journal Sentinel. “It doesn’t necessarily have to be with me being the main guy and all that. If it is me, great. But I want to be there again. If that’s going to be me being there as a role player, if that’s going to me being there as the fifth option, if that’s going to be me being there as the No. 1 guy that takes them there, I don’t give two (expletive). I want to get there again."

item.title: Ryan Conwell heads to Miami at No. 37 via OKC trade
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: Oklahoma City is trading No. 37 Ryan Conwell to the Miami Heat for No. 41 and cash.

item.title: Celtics offers Bucks Jaylen Brown, two first rounders for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Shams Charania: The Celtics aggressively pursued Giannis, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, sources tell ESPN. The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: Miami and Boston, which were both on Antetokounmpo’s list of preferred destinations.

item.title: Smart move considering Miami is likely facing …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Smart move considering Miami is likely facing financial restrictions filling out their bench. The Heat are $18M below the first apron with 4-5 roster spots available.

item.title: "I had a great workout, put my best foot forward." He …
item.description: Barry Jackson: In ongoing media session, Ryan Conwell said he worked out for the Heat before the draft: "I had a great workout, put my best foot forward." He said "my ability to space the floor, my shooting ability, drawing some attention, my competitiveness" will help the team.

item.title: Tre Donaldson signs with Miami on two-way
item.description: Shams Charania: Undrafted Miami guard Tre Donaldson has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Miami Heat, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way …
item.description: Michael Scotto‬: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way contract with MAC Player of the Year Peter Suder. He led Miami (OH) to a 31-0 regular-season record and shot 42% from 3-point range this season. Keith Kreiter and Sam Cipriano of Edge Sports completed the deal.

item.title: Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "The most important thing in the league right now for what is going to happen in the 2026-27 season is that Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown probably needs to be traded for them to be competitive. Because that is now going to be the next storyline in the NBA. Obviously, the Heat getting Giannis is a huge story for them. From the moment they traded Jimmy Butler, they have been on the hunt for the next star. That is a huge story for them."

item.title: At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full …
item.description: Beyond a pair of double doors inside the lower-level bowl of Fiserv Forum, a hallway leads to the Bucks locker room. Following the signing of his maximum contract extension in the winter of 2020, it was adorned with Antetokounmpo’s Nike logo, an interlocking “GA." At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full campaign as head coach, the “GA” was removed. Instead, the team emblazoned the word “together” in different languages. The English version of the word was misspelled.

item.title: J’Vonne Hadley has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: Louisville’s J’Vonne Hadley has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Miami Heat, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’7” swingman is an efficient (61.9 2P%, 44.0 3P%) glue guy and natural complementary player. Helped lead his team to the Portsmouth Invitational title.

item.title: Heat adding Meechie Johnson Jr. to summer league team
item.description: Barry Jackson: Heat is adding South Carolina PG Meechie Johnson Jr. to its summer league team, per source. Averaged 17.2 points, 4.3 assists last season. Previously played at Ohio State.

item.title: Sources say he has a similar view regarding a …
item.description: And sources say that Leonard would not have interest in signing an extension with Detroit in the event that the Pistons traded for him this summer. Sources say he has a similar view regarding a long-term partnership with the Heat if Miami had any designs on pursuing Leonard to slot alongside Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo.

item.title: For those who have questioned Heat willingness to …
item.description: Ira Winderman: For those who have questioned Heat willingness to spend, they paid $1.5 million to move up four spots in Wednesday night's second round. Adam Simon, "Ownership gave us the opportunity to move up. So we appreciate them doing that."

item.title: Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free …
item.description: Former University of Kansas men’s basketball players Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free agent deals with NBA teams after not being selected in the two-round NBA Draft, KU confirmed in a pair of posts on social media site X on Thursday morning. White, a 6-foot-7 wing from Dallas, has signed a deal with the Miami Heat. He initially reported the news with 247Sports via a social media post. Council, a 6-4 guard from Rochester, New York, has signed with the New Orleans Pelicans. Counci

item.title: Dan Gilbert on reuniting with LeBron James: 'If you're both mutually on the same mission, it can work out'
item.description: Interviewer: How did two such strong personalities as Dan Gilbert—owner of the Cleveland Cavs, mega-billionaire, business owner, operator—and LeBron James have that kind of falling out and then make it work again? Dan Gilbert: Well, I flew to Miami, where he was with two or three of his advisers, and we had a couple-hour meeting. I think, at the end of the day, it was in both of our interests to do this because he wanted to come back. He wanted to come back home, and we’re the only place he coul

item.title: Dan Gilbert: Yeah. And it was stupid overall. If I had …
item.description: Interviewer: You wrote a very strongly worded letter. Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: I’m not going to make you read it. Dan Gilbert: No. Interviewer: But it talked about betrayal. Dan Gilbert: Well, what happened was, remember, he went on national television and did a show about where he was going to go play next. Interviewer: Yeah. Is that where you found out, though? Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: You found out when he announced it? Dan Gilbert: Yes. They never told us in advance. Interviewer:

item.title: So two people read it. Were you counseled not to put …
item.description: Interviewer: You gave him some heat. What were you thinking? Was that you doing it? Did some intern draft that letter? Dan Gilbert: I actually—you should have seen the first version. No, I’m kidding. Interviewer: Wait, did you have other people read this letter? Dan Gilbert: Yeah, I had one or two people, but they didn’t tell me what they should have told me. Interviewer: Who were the one or two people? Your brother? Dan Gilbert: No, one of the PR people. And I think maybe my ex-wife read it. In

item.title: Dan Gilbert reveals Comic Sans in his LeBron James letter was a joke by a team member weeks earlier
item.description: Well, the whole conversation around it was also around the font choice of the letter. Dan Gilbert: That was another thing. Interviewer: Comic Sans. Dan Gilbert: What was weird is that somebody on my team, just as a joke, I think the week or two before, changed my font to Comic Sans. Interviewer: What was your original font going to be? Dan Gilbert: Probably Times New Roman or something. So it wasn’t changed just for that letter. It was changed weeks ahead of that. Interviewer: So everything was

item.title: The Heat has added 7-1 Arnas Sakenis to its summer …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Heat has added 7-1 Arnas Sakenis to its summer league program. Was 11th in the nation in blocked shots per game (2.34) for Southern Illinois University Edwardsville.

item.title: Damjan Rudez on Andrew Wiggins: He doesn't have that high-energy drive that separates the superstars from the great players
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'I cannot accurately describe how immense Andrew Wiggins' potential truly was. Having been on the court with him, I believe he had more raw potential than KAT, Zach LaVine, or 99% of the players I ever played with—even including guys like Paul George. There was a dunk he had against Atlanta where I swear I was looking at Michael Jordan. He would just rise and keep rising over three defenders. However, he didn't always have the 'motor'—that high-energy drive that separates the super

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was 'genuinely torn' between wanting to be traded or stay in Milwaukee
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo was genuinely torn, multiple sources close to him say. Between loyalty and love of a city that had embraced him as a skinny kid from Greece 13 years ago, and his desire to compete for more championships with a franchise that had exhausted all its resources to try to do that for him. Those contradictory forces had swirled for years.

item.title: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding …
item.description: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding offers and presenting them to his front office and ownership group, then getting feedback from Antetokounmpo's camp as to whether he'd be willing to extend his contract with those teams. At least four strong bids had died on the vine after Antetokounmpo rejected them, multiple sources with knowledge of them say.

item.title: But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple …
item.description: THE BUCKS WERE pressing up against the deadline co-owner Jimmy Haslam had set a month earlier. During a news conference to introduce new coach Taylor Jenkins and sitting alongside Horst, Haslam had made a declaration: The Bucks would have Antetokounmpo's future settled by draft night. Either he would agree to sign an extension to remain with Milwaukee -- or he'd be traded. But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple sources with knowledge of the situation say. Antetokounmpo was not going t

item.title: Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of …
item.description: For half a decade, the Bucks were one of the most successful franchises in the league, leading the NBA in winning percentage (.686) across the regular season and playoffs from 2018 to 2023. Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of the organization through the years: Teams don't willingly trade superstars, especially in a small market such as Milwaukee. It's why the Bucks had made every attempt, through multiple roster iterations, to build championship-level teams with Antetokounmpo

item.title: Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks …
item.description: Later that summer, Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks management their concerns over the roster, and asked the team to explore a trade to the New York Knicks. Talks stalled.

item.title: In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come …
item.description: HORST SLEPT WELL Sunday night and longer than he usually does, relieved to be on the other side of the most consequential decision of his professional life. In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come by. Both franchises believed they had a great chance of landing Antetokounmpo, according to team sources, but they were paralyzed until a final decision had been made.

item.title: Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal
item.description: THROUGHOUT THE DAY on Monday, the Bucks had gone back and forth over the two offers. There were "spirited debates" and differences of opinion, as one Bucks source put it. But in the end, they preferred the optionality that Miami's package gave them in building something new. Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal.

item.title: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from …
item.description: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from Milwaukee back to his native Greece, it seemed to be a signal that the emotional cord between the city and the man who had become a star in it had been severed. Antetokounmpo could never bring himself to publicly ask for a trade, but moving his family to Greece and enrolling his children in Greek schools seemed to be his way of doing so. The move didn't last long, though. Antetokounmpo's family was back in Milwaukee within a few months, multipl

item.title: "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed …
item.description: The second was the inclusion of Holiday in the trade that brought superstar Lillard from Portland. "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed everything was put Jrue in that trade," a Bucks source said. "Jrue was such a leader on and off the floor. He would do things like if Giannis was holding the ball too much, Jrue would just bring it up and play with everybody else without making it a thing. He was a pro at it. "But Jrue was in the deal [for Lillard] because we thought we needed

item.title: “I can see him [LeBron James] coming back. It’ll …
item.description: The Lakers made a check-in call to LeBron James early in free agency and haven’t followed up with an offer since. That silence is opening a door Pat Riley may be ready to walk through. The 2x Miami Heat champion Mario Chalmers will never stop rooting for a LeBron reunion in South Florida. Now the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade has rallied him to suggest that LeBron James and his son, Bronny James, could make a shocking move to the Heat. Appearing on WQAM radio, Chalmers was asked what blockbuster a

item.title: “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG …
item.description: Chalmers implied that Bronny would be part of this potential move. He went on to highlight how the infrastructure in Miami would serve as the perfect environment to nurture Bronny’s development. “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG and keep developing him and let him learn from other guys, so I think it’s a great move.”

item.title: Miami to decline $2.4 million team option on Jahmir Young
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Miami Heat will decline the $2.41 million team option on Jahmir Young, sources told @hoopshype . Young made All-NBA G League Second Team, averaging 25.9 points, 8.5 assists, 4.9 rebounds, and 1.8 steals last season. He’s played 20 NBA games for the Bulls and Heat in two years.

item.title: It’s unclear whether Young will still be on the Heat’s …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Can confirm that the Heat has declined Jahmir Young’s option for next season. He’s now an unrestricted free agent. It’s unclear whether Young will still be on the Heat’s summer league roster.

item.title: Heat not expected to add Thanasis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat WILL NOT be taking anymore Antetokounmpo brothers on their roster, per @ramonashelburne “There won’t be anymore Antetokounmpo brothers in Miami. That whole ‘we have Kostas and Thanasis on the team’ I don’t think that’s going to continue from what I understand.” (Via ESPN LA YouTube)

item.title: Heat have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Miami Heat have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Vladislav Goldin, a league source told @spotrac. Goldin will now be a restricted free agent this summer.

item.title: Can confirm from NBA source the Heat have tendered a …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Can confirm from NBA source the Heat have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Vlad Goldin. The Heat are expected to bypass a similar move with two-way player Trevor Keels. Goldin is now guaranteed at least a two-way salary for 2026-27 but still can accept a standard deal from another team.

item.title: The Heat is expected to bypass a two-way QO for Trevor …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Can confirm the Heat has extended a two-way qualifying offer to Vlad Goldin. Goldin is now guaranteed at least a two-way salary for this upcoming season, but can still accept a standard deal from another team. The Heat is expected to bypass a two-way QO for Trevor Keels, who would then become an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: Serbia secured a convincing 90-76 victory over Czechia …
item.description: Serbia secured a convincing 90-76 victory over Czechia in a closed-door friendly at Aleksandar Nikolić Hall in Belgrade as part of its preparations for the upcoming FIBA window.

item.title: Nikola Jović led Serbia with 20 points, adding five …
item.description: Nikola Jović led Serbia with 20 points, adding five rebounds and three assists. Nikola Jokić finished with 16 points, 14 rebounds and eight assists, narrowly missing a triple-double, while Dejan Davidovac contributed 14 points. Vít Krejčí paced Czechia with 16 points and four assists.

item.title: LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James felt like ESPN took advantage of him in 2010 when he decided to join the Heat, per @WindhorstESPN “The Heat did not like us there. LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN had turned his back on him and taken advantage of him in the wake of the decision. He was annoyed by all the coverage. Looking back on it, it was annoying.”

item.title: Mavs interested in adding Willie Green to Dusty May's staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Former Pelicans coach Willie Green, who has been pursued by Golden State to rejoin Steve Kerr's staff as an assistant, also has interest from Dallas as new Mavericks coach Dusty May assembles his first NBA staff, league sources say. Former Bucks coach Adrian Griffin and Heat consultant Noah LaRoche have likewise been mentioned as expected candidates for May's bench in Dallas. As @TheSteinLine previously reported, LaRoche was part of the monthlong interview process that eventually led

item.title: The Pistons were linked with interest in free agent …
item.description: Regarding other potential acquisitions, the Pistons were linked with interest in free agent Norman Powell and Kawhi Leonard as a trade suitor. However, that chatter has lessened over the past few days following the Joe acquisition and rumblings that Leonard wouldn’t want to extend with Detroit within league circles.

item.title: Aaron Gordon has drawn trade interest from Blazers; Celtics have kept tabs
item.description: Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon was considered among the contingency plans for the Heat if the franchise didn’t land Giannis Antetokounmpo via trade, league sources told HoopsHype. Similarly, the Portland Trail Blazers have expressed trade interest in Gordon should they not land Jaylen Brown, their preferred target, HoopsHype has learned. Ironically, the Celtics have also kept tabs on Gordon from afar.

item.title: Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from Lakers, Celtics, Heat, Clippers, Magic
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Nuggets navigate getting below the second apron, sharpshooting forward Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Los Angeles Lakers, Boston Celtics, Miami Heat, Los Angeles Clippers, and Orlando Magic, league sources told HoopsHype. The 30-year-old forward shot career-highs from the field (.480) and beyond the arc (.430), and is on an expiring $23.06 million contract for the upcoming season.

item.title: Dru Smith's $2.6 million salary for next season …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Dru Smith's $2.6 million salary for next season becomes fully guaranteed if he's still on the Heat's roster past 11:59 p.m. today.

item.title: Nikola Jovic says Tyler Herro was in 'every single' Heat trade rumor
item.description: Nikola Jovic: ‘Ever since I arrived in Miami, I have been in trade rumors for Kevin Durant twice. Yes, twice for KD. For Kyrie Irving twice. For Damian Lillard, it was already a done deal because Lillard publicly stated that he wanted to come to Miami. It was like, 'I want Miami,' and that was it. For Lillard! And now most recently, I was in there for Giannis. I have been in seven or eight trade rumors already. Me and my brother Tyler Herro. I mean, my brother Tyler Herro, what he has endured, i

item.title: Heat targeting Tim Hardaway Jr. and Mike Conley
item.description: League sources say that the Heat will have strong interest in the veteran duo of Tim Hardaway Jr. and Mike Conley Jr.

item.title: LeBron James has not expressed interest in returning to Heat yet
item.description: Speculation continues to swirl about whether LeBron James could have interest in a return to the Heat. Nothing should be ruled out, but this is not something that has been actively pursued by James to this point, according to a source. Miami very likely would explore if James decides to pursue it.

item.title: Heat linked to Khris Middleton
item.description: Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA forward Khris Middleton, who played with soon-to-be-new-Heat-forward Giannis Antetekounmpo in Milwaukee.

item.title: The Heat is expected to try to add at least two …
item.description: The Heat is expected to try to add at least two skilled three-point shooters in free agency. Other options, listed here, include Landry Shamet, Anfernee Simons and Quentin Grimes, among others.

item.title: I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim …
item.description: Barry Jackson: With regard to the Heat report by the very hard-working @TheSteinLine and @JakeLFischer , I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim Hardaway Jr. when free agency begins Tuesday night, per a source. This would be a very sensible marriage if it happens... With regard to Mike Conley, he has received some consideration internally. But there are some internally who would prefer going a younger route.

item.title: Mavs also interested in Cam Johnson
item.description: Rival teams consistently point to Nuggets forward Cam Johnson, acquired last summer in a deal that sent away Michael Porter Jr., as the most likely candidate to be dealt, in large part because the 30-year-old has just one season left on his deal at $23 million. Miami and Dallas are believed to be among the many interested teams.

item.title: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of …
item.description: Barry Jackson: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of Bobby Marks saying he would be perfect fit at the minimum for Heat, feedback I've received is that he will enter free agency looking for more than that, understandably (averaged 14.9 ppg, 38.1 on 3s in career). There are always players who fall through cracks and take minimums (like Hardaway with Denver last year, Oubre with 76ers two years ago, etc. etc.). Heat will be poised to pounce if the right player falls through the cra

item.title: Ja Morant expected to be traded this week
item.description: Sources say the Heat had interest in Grizzlies point guard Ja Morant before the Giannis deal, but there is not much expectation that he will get bought out. In fact, league sources expect Morant to be traded this week.

item.title: League sources say the Heat have shopped Nikola Jović …
item.description: League sources say the Heat have shopped Nikola Jović in an attempt to cut salary to open flexibility for Powell. But Jović is entering the first year of a four-year, $62 million contract that already looks like an albatross by role player standards given his lack of development at 23 years old. Miami would have to attach draft capital to Jović’s contract to dump him, but the front office only has a 2029 first and swaps in 2028 and 2032 to dangle.

item.title: Tim Hardaway Jr., whose father Tim Sr. partnered with …
item.description: Impending free agent guard Tim Hardaway Jr., whose father Tim Sr. partnered with Alonzo Mourning as linchpins on the Heat’s best teams of the 20th century, has emerged as an expected Heat target in the coming days. The Heat will have interest in Hardaway when free agency begins at 6 p.m. on Tuesday, according to a source. He’s among a select group of skilled shooters that Miami is expected to call. Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA

item.title: Heat to guarantee Dru Smith's salary
item.description: The Heat will guarantee Smith’s full $2.6 million salary for next season, according to a league source. Miami had a Sunday deadline of 11:59 p.m. to waive Smith and shed his $2.6 million salary cap hit for this upcoming season.

item.title: Celtics were already shopping Jaylen Brown before Giannis talks heated up?
item.description: Brown hasn't said how he feels about the Celtics openly shopping him in trade scenarios, which sources have said took place with other teams even before the talks for Antetokounmpo heated up. The Celtics have three choices: (1) trade Brown to another team, which so far hasn't yielded strong offers; (2) extend Brown, hoping the financial commitment will assuage any hard feelings; or (3) bring him back without an extension and try to make him comfortable continuing on in this state of limbo.

item.title: The Heat's newest addition spent last season playing …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Heat's newest addition spent last season playing for Clemson's football team. Ian Schieffelin, a 6-8 forward, accepted an offer to play on the Heat's summer league team. He started 99 games and appeared in 134 for Clemson's basketball team over 4 years (averaged 12.4 points, 9.4 rebounds in 2024-25), then played football last season after using up his hoops eligibility. He caught 2 passes for 10 yards, playing 20 snaps for Dabo Swinney's team.

item.title: Andrew Wiggins opts into player option for 2026-27 with Miami, signs three-year, $64 million contract with Heat
item.description: Jake Fischer: Miami Heat swingman Andrew Wiggins has opted in for his $30.2 million salary this 2026-27 season, sources say.

item.title: Miami Heat forward Andrew Wiggins intends to a sign a …
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Miami Heat forward Andrew Wiggins intends to a sign a three-year, $64 million contract to stay with the franchise, with a player option in the third season, sources tell ESPN. Heat executives and Wiggins' agents at CAA sports Steven Heumann and Andrew Morrison, as well as Richard Clarke, came to terms on a critical commitment.

item.title: Wiggins will serve as a key starter alongside Heat …
item.description: Shams Charania: Wiggins will serve as a key starter alongside Heat stars Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo -- and Miami received his commitment on a $64M deal through 2028-29 (player option in 2028). He opts in for $30M in 2026-27 as part of the deal.

item.title: It'll be much harder to re-sign Norman Powell without …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Miami Heat salary cap situation with Andrew Wiggins opting in, Pelle Larsson opted in, Dru Smith guaranteed, and Jahmir Young opted out. They have $20.5 million under the hard cap to fill five roster spots. It'll be much harder to re-sign Norman Powell without additional moves.

item.title: Ben Simmons planning NBA comeback, mentions Miami and Philadelphia as potential landing spots
item.description: The plan is to stay in L.A. for a few days and then return to Miami, where he’ll ramp up his training: two and a half to three hours of lifting and body work, as well as hour-and-a-half sessions on the court, with a day or two off every week. He hopes to be back to full strength by his 30th birthday, July 20. He’s now an unrestricted free agent, and I ask him explicitly if he’s planning to play in the NBA next year. “I plan on getting as strong as I can physically, getting my ass on the court, a

item.title: So, Wiggins will make $30.2 million this upcoming …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: So, Wiggins will make $30.2 million this upcoming season. Then he agreed a two-year, $34 million extension through the 2028-29 season.

item.title: This gets him under contract for $17 M range next …
item.description: Barry Jackson: OK, clarity on all this, from @Anthony_Chiang and me: Wiggins will get $30 M this season; he opted in (as @JakeFischer said). This gets him under contract for $17 M range next season.

item.title: So Heat can use about $12 M of its $15.1 M midlevel …
item.description: Barry Jackson: So Heat can use about $12 M of its $15.1 M midlevel exception this season and remain under $209 M hard cap - presuming the other players they sign are on minimum deals. Wiggins' 3 year, $64 M deal includes an opt out for 2028-29. So Heat gets Wiggins guaranteed for 2 years in front-loaded deal.

item.title: According to NBA source the major giveback from …
item.description: Ira Winderman: According to NBA source the major giveback from Wiggins is in the first and second years of that two year-extension, taking a reduction to $16.5 million in the first year and then $17.5 million in second year. So the saving vs. cap/tax not immediate, but rather down the road.

item.title: Sam Amick: Celtics are asking for the sun and the moon in Jaylen Brown trade talks
item.description: Sam Amick on Jaylen Brown: "This one is, if I had to handicap it, it just feels like somebody has got to put enough on the table to get this thing done, but Boston is asking for the sun and the moon. I keep looking at Portland and because people keep bringing Portland up when I talk to them, they wanted to go big on Giannis Antetokounmpo and that did not work. Even without a commitment, they were willing to try to do a Giannis trade. And when you see that kind of boldness, then you kind of go, a

item.title: While the Heat's intention is to keep Andrew Wiggins, …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: While the Heat's intention is to keep Andrew Wiggins, he is still immediately eligible to be traded even after agreeing and eventually signing his two-year extension.

item.title: The deadline for the Heat to extend a qualifying offer …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: The deadline for the Heat to extend a qualifying offer to Keshad Johnson is today. But it looks like the Heat will bypass that qualifying offer and Johnson will become an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: Indications are Johnson will spend summer-league time …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat have one personnel decision remaining today, with it now looking as if there will not be a qualifying offer to Keshad Johnson, making him an unrestricted free agent Tuesday. Indications are Johnson will spend summer-league time with the Heat.

item.title: Heat looking to sign-and-trade Norman Powell?
item.description: Heat Central: The Heat are looking to trade Nikola Jovic in addition to a sign and trade for Norman Powell, per @JakeLFischer “Nikola Jovic, his deal is definitely put out there on the trade market… He is a piece in addition to trying to sign and trade Norman Powell to a different destination”

item.title: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat, there’s a growing sense within league circles that All-Star guard Norman Powell won’t return. The Chicago Bulls are among the teams expected to pursue Powell as free agency nears, as first reported in my story Sunday.

item.title: For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Powell can simply sign into Bulls cap space if he chooses. For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more money' -- answer is it's a very hard contract to move and the Heat historically has been opposed to attaching first rounders to a negative asset simply to get off money. That has been a bridge too far for them over the years.

item.title: Norman Powell isn't expecting a sign-and-trade out of …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Norman Powell isn't expecting a sign-and-trade out of Miami, per a source familiar with where things stand there. Of course, much can change, but that's the intel at this point.

item.title: There was some hope that Wiggins would opt out of his …
item.description: The Heat intends to keep Wiggins as part of this upcoming season’s roster, according to a league source, but he’s still immediately eligible to be traded even after he eventually signs his two-year extension. There was some hope that Wiggins would opt out of his player option to renegotiate his deal into a longer contract with a lower starting salary, giving the Heat more flexibility to operate in free agency this offseason.

item.title: Davion Mitchell will now wear No. 2 for the Heat to …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Davion Mitchell will now wear No. 2 for the Heat to honor a late friend, Spud. “We both came from the 912, and he will always be a part of who I am. Wearing this number is my way of keeping his memory close and carrying a piece of him with me every time I step on the court.”

item.title: Simone Fontecchio to return to Miami on one-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent Simone Fontecchio intends to sign a one-year deal to return to the Miami Heat, sources tell ESPN. Heat officials worked with Fontecchio's agent at Excel Sports Management, Sam Goldfeder, to bring back the shooter who averaged 8.5 points and 37.5% 3-point shooting.

item.title: Simone Fontecchio’s actual salary will be $2.8 million …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Simone Fontecchio’s actual salary will be $2.8 million for next season.

item.title: Overall Giannis getting to go to one of the places he …
item.description: Draymond Green: As it stands today, I don’t think it leaves Giannis in a much better position next year than he would’ve been with the Bucks. However, the long run I think definitely leaves them in a better position because you can build that team out. We saw even when LeBron and Chris Bosh went to the Miami Heat, that team looked a bit different the second year than the first year. Overall Giannis getting to go to one of the places he preferred to go to, I think it’s great.

item.title: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for …
item.description: Barry Jackson: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for Miami when the Heat opted not to create full mid-level money in recent days. They could create full $15.1 mid-level money, if LeBron somewhat surprisingly decides to leave the West and come here, by attaching a draft pick to Jovic with hope of finding a taker.

item.title: LeBron James to Miami has been discussed around the league as a possibility
item.description: Heat Central: REPORT: LeBron James has been linked to the Miami Heat after the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade, per @JakeLFischer “He’s been connected to Miami, a potential homecoming there once Giannis was traded to South Beach. There’s plenty talk around the league that they could see that reunion”

item.title: Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors expected to emerge as suitors for LeBron James
item.description: SportsCenter: I'm told this is a happiness-led decision for LeBron James. ... Wherever he lands in free agency, it will not be driven by money." "It's unclear what the timeline will be. Right now, he's in Ohio spending time with his family. He's gonna make a decision on his own time. But there's no question all of the NBA's contending teams are in the picture for LeBron James. Rich Paul will call LeBron with expressed interest as soon as free agency opens up tonight. Around the league, teams exp

item.title: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the …
item.description: Heat Central: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the Cavs, and you look at the Heat. He’s been in those places. He knows the terrain. I think it’s too soon to say anyone is the front runner” — @TheSteinLine on LeBron destinations

item.title: Heat summer roster does not include Keshad Johnson, …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat summer roster does not include Keshad Johnson, but does include Myron Gardner, Vlad Goldin, Jahmir Young and Trevor Keels. Of that group, only Gardner presently under contract to Heat. Former Pacer and Net Kendall Brown also on Heat summer roster.

item.title: The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James main priority is going to a team that has a chance at winning, per @ChrisBHaynes "I'm told he's going to be open minded and patient for his next team.... The one component a team is going to have to have to secure his service from what i'm told, is the team has to be competitive and have a realistic chance at winning.... The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can pretty much look and see of those 3 who has the best shot at winning... Money wasn't mentioned"

item.title: Timberwolves have interest in LeBron James?
item.description: Cleveland and Miami are often cited as realistic landing spots as well, and the Minnesota Timberwolves are known to have interest if he felt like shocking the basketball world with that sort of move.

item.title: Bones Hyland returning to Minnesota
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Bones Hyland has agreed to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves next season, sources told @TheAthletic. Hyland established himself as a rotation player, teaming with Ayo Dosunmu in the "Twin Turbos" backcourt.

item.title: “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have interest in LeBron James, per @TheSteinLine “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but there is a line…. Cleveland is certainly going to try and convince him of a 3rd marriage, Miami has interest…. This might not necessarily happen tonight”

item.title: Hardaway agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million deal with …
item.description: Shams Charania: Hardaway agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million deal with the Heat, sources said. Miami officials finalized the deal with Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports tonight.

item.title: Tim Hardaway Jr signs with Miami
item.description: Chris Haynes: Free agent sharpshooter Tim Hardaway Jr. will be signing with the Miami Heat to provide the organization with the spacing needed for Giannis Antetokounmpo, league sources tell me.

item.title: Heat using a portion of its midlevel exception on …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Tim Hardaway Jr. agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million contract to join the Heat, a league source confirms. Heat using a portion of its midlevel exception on Hardaway.

item.title: And Hardaway agrees to terms with Heat. Miami zeroed …
item.description: Barry Jackson: And Hardaway agrees to terms with Heat. Miami zeroed in on Hardaway from the start. Played 80 games last season and 3rd in 6th man voting. Read all about him below.

item.title: "You're going to be looking for guys on minimum deals …
item.description: Heat Central: Chris Haynes on what's next for the Miami Heat after signing Tim Hardaway Jr.: "You're going to be looking for guys on minimum deals who can be available on the cheap for 1 year deals where the benefit for them is they can come in and maybe have a really good season that contributes to a high success of winning, then hit the market the following summer to increase their value. The thing about Miami and Giannis, It's going to be an attractive place."

item.title: Windhorst: If you want LeBron James attention, go out and make a move to improve your team
item.description: Heat Central: "If you want LeBron, you got to go make a move. I don't mean bringing LeBron in.... If you want LeBron James attention, make a move. Make a move to improve your team and then you get his attention" - @WindhorstESPN on Lebron 👀 If LeBron truly isn't worried about the money, the Heat CAN'T be ruled out.

item.title: "I didn't even think about that until you asked me …
item.description: Bennett Durando: First conversation I had with Tim last year, I asked him if any part of his reason for signing with Denver was the track record of guys getting paid more after playing with Jokic. "I didn't even think about that until you asked me that question."

item.title: “When I talk to teams, there’s a few that really stand …
item.description: Heat Central: “When I talk to teams, there’s a few that really stand out. The Miami Heat are definitely one of them. I expect the Heat to have interest. You have Giannis & Bam. You have history with LeBron. Warm weather. The market of Miami where he already won 2 championships. That will obviously come calling” — @ShamsCharania on LeBron 👀

item.title: "It's an opportunity for him to explore his game a …
item.description: The Spurs also want Bryant to spread his wings offensively this summer. Williamson said the 14th overall pick of the 2025 NBA Draft will only see action in Las Vegas, where the Spurs will play at least five games after kicking off the summer circuit with three games at the California Classic in San Francisco. It all begins against the Miami Heat at 7 p.m. Friday at Chase Center, home of the Golden State Warriors. "It's an opportunity for him to explore his game a little bit without having to wor

item.title: Erik Spoelstra and LeBron James really bonded during …
item.description: Heat Central: Erik Spoelstra and LeBron James really bonded during their time together on team USA in 2024, per @WindhorstESPN "We should point out the team USA connections to the Miami Heat. Bam Adebayo was a huge part of the 2024 USA squad. Erik Spoelstra and LeBron really bonded during that summer.... Erik Spoelstra was LeBron's personal workout guy during that 6 weeks over the summer. Soon as the court opened before the game, the first 2 people on the court were Spoelstra and LeBron. I think

item.title: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Miami Heat in …
item.description: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Miami Heat in 2026-27 eased from 15.9% to 12.9% on Polymarket today.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James in no rush to make decision
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James future: “One quote really sticks out to me. Rich Paul on June 2025 to me, ‘LeBron James wants to compete for a championship, he knows the Lakers are building for the future.’ When I talk to teams around the league it’s a few teams that really get narrowed. It’s the Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers, and the Miami Heat as a perspective destination. I’m told LeBron it’s not in a rush.”

item.title: “If you are a team with contending aspirations, LeBron …
item.description: Heat Central: “If you are a team with contending aspirations, LeBron James according to my sources it is not a financially driven decision. If you want to slot him in and pitch him on a minimum, an exception, pitch him in a non max contract, there seems to be a window there.” — @ShamsCharania on LeBron not making money his priority

item.title: LeBron James and the Lakers ‘simply didn't see eye to …
item.description: NBA Base: LeBron James and the Lakers ‘simply didn't see eye to eye’ on what it meant to compete for a championship, per @ShamsCharania LeBron's next move will not be financially driven and is based entirely on his happiness and playing “meaningful basketball.” Last summer, Rich Paul basically challenged the Lakers' desire to win. Now, the Warriors, Cavs, and Heat are the top suitors.

item.title: Starting today, the Heat can sign Simone Fontecchio to …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Starting today, the Heat can sign Simone Fontecchio to his minimum contract and also sign second-round pick Ryan Conwell to his first NBA contract.

item.title: There’s already a history with both Miami and …
item.description: Heat Central: Shams explains why Miami would be enticing to LeBron: “They have Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo, and Erik Spoelstra. They’re the Miami Heat. There’s already a history with both Miami and Cleveland if LeBron wants to go to either destination and go back to the Eastern conference. But there’s a reason GS formulated a plan over the last week that included Draymond Green surprisingly opting out”

item.title: The structure of Powell’s deal gives Chicago …
item.description: After extending center Collins on Tuesday, the Bulls entered Day 2 of free agency with around $23 million in salary-cap space and the room exception of $9.4 million. With Norman Powell’s deal, the Bulls are essentially left to operate with that room exception. The structure of Powell’s deal gives Chicago flexibility to pivot after this season. It also gives it the chance to potentially capitalize on Powell’s value at the trade deadline.

item.title: Norman Powell, Bulls agree to a two-year, $45 million contract
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Chicago Bulls and Norman Powell have agreed to a two-year, $45 million deal. Powell is coming off his first All-Star season and is a career 40% 3-point shooter.

item.title: Relationship between Heat, LeBron James now 'strong'
item.description: The relationship between the Heat and LeBron James initially was frosty after their breakup, but is now strong. The Heat would welcome a return by James, a source has said. Even at 41, James remains a very effective player. He averaged 20.9 points, 6.1 rebounds and 7.2 assists per game while shooting 51.5% from the field and 31.7% on threes in 60 appearances for the Lakers last season.

item.title: If LeBron James wants to return to the Heat, the team …
item.description: If LeBron James wants to return to the Heat, the team would have interest in making that happen, a league source conveyed on Tuesday. James, who is entering his 24th NBA season, has a history with the Heat. James spent four seasons with the Heat from 2010 to 2014, winning NBA championships with the organization in 2012 and 2013 in four NBA Finals appearances. He also was selected as the NBA’s Most Valuable Player two times while with the Heat.

item.title: "The LeBron James thing to Miami is real ... They've …
item.description: The Dan Le Batard Show: "The LeBron James thing to Miami is real ... They've got a plan around Giannis that's about this year."

item.title: Khris Middleton remains a possibility for one of those …
item.description: Veteran Khris Middleton remains a possibility for one of those minimum contracts. Miami has respect for veteran point guard Gabe Vincent, who was on the Heat’s 2023 Finals team, but it’s undecided if the Heat will pursue this. Either way, adding another point guard remains a necessity.

item.title: “When it became official, it was great. We are very …
item.description: The first Tim Hardaway to play for the Miami Heat is thrilled that another Tim Hardaway is joining the Heat. “When it became official, it was great. We are very happy,” Tim Hardaway Sr. said of his son joining an organization that he once starred for. “We’ve been waiting on this for a long time.”

item.title: “A couple of times we tried to get him there, but it …
item.description: The agreement brings the 34-year-old Hardaway Jr. back to Miami, where he grew up and graduated from Palmetto High School before playing at Michigan in college and then being drafted by the New York Knicks with the 24th overall pick in 2013. Hardaway Jr. still lives in Miami during the NBA offseason. “A couple of times we tried to get him there, but it just didn’t work,” Hardaway Sr. said. “It just didn’t work. This time it worked out. And he’s happy. We’re happy. He’s at home, he’s where he wan

item.title: “I’m happy because they play the right way,” Hardaway …
item.description: Hardaway Sr. believes his son will fit in well with the Heat as a three-point shooting floor spacer around its leading duo of Bam Adebayo and Giannis Antetokounmpo. “I’m happy because they play the right way,” Hardaway Sr. said. “Erik Spoelstra makes sure they play the right way. They make sure that they get in offense, run offense. The first priority is three-point shooters. Get your shooters off. Let’s get a shot as quickly as we can first and see if we get an easy shot. And then after that, l

item.title: Ryan Conwell deal with Heat is three years starting at …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Ryan Conwell deal with Heat is three years starting at $1.36 million for 2026-27, with 50% guarantee on second year, and conditional guarantee in third, then restricted free agency. Same as Pelle Larsson deal.

item.title: Spurs not expected to go after LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Spurs’ deal with Tobias Harris slams the point home, but league sources say San Antonio is not expected to pursue LeBron James. @BennettDurando reported that Denver, by contrast, IS joining Golden State, Cleveland and Miami in this chase. More NBA: tinyurl.com/pebetpkn

item.title: Miami Heat remain in the mix for LeBron James, Khris …
item.description: NBACentral: The Miami Heat remain in the mix for LeBron James, Khris Middleton, Bradley Beal, and Anfernee Simons, per @JakeLFischer (Via @BleacherReport )

item.title: Heat have spoken with Bradley Beal since free agency started
item.description: The Heat has had discussions with the representation for free agent guard Bradley Beal in the first 24 hours of free agency, according to a league source

item.title: By today they were actively shopping full blown …
item.description: Heat Central: "For the Celtics where this had come down to is they tried to trade for Giannis.. They missed out. Ever since then what's been interesting, they didn't stop the conversations there. By today they were actively shopping full blown shopping Jaylen Brown around the league... Jaylen Brown never asked out of Boston. This was all the Celtics" - @ShamsCharania

item.title: While Middleton hasn’t been ruled out, he was not …
item.description: Veteran wing Khris Middleton has been linked to the Heat in free agency, largely because of his connection with Giannis Antetokounmpo. While Middleton hasn’t been ruled out, he was not targeted by the Heat early in free agency, according to a league source. Middleton, 34, remains an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: I'm happy that he's doing it, but no, he can't wear …
item.description: The Hall of Famer talked about the possibility of allowing his son to don his retired number on the Hochman, Crowder and Solana Show: "My legacy is my legacy. Even though he likes to wear number 10, that is not coming down from the rafters." When reflecting on the significance of having a jersey retired, he said: "That is tough to do. And when it's up there, you want it to stay up there and you don't want nobody to touch it," the legendary guard added. "And I love him, I love him to death, I'm h

item.title: LeBron and his agent controlling the sport. In the …
item.description: Dan Le Batard: "And the story I've told before of Pat Riley in a room having to wait to meet with LeBron, LeBron's what Phil Jackson would call LeBron's posse because this was a power game between the Phil Jacksons and Pat Rileys getting to control the sport and LeBron and his agent controlling the sport. In the room where he was recruiting them, he had to ask them to turn off the television because they were distracted while he was doing the recruiting. And all of that ended up being publicly h

item.title: I asked associate about Heat and Beal and his response …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I asked associate about Heat and Beal and his response was "we'll see. The Heat would be an interesting spot."

item.title: While Hardaway Jr. will wear a Heat uniform, he won’t …
item.description: Tim Hardaway Jr. is heading back to Miami — the city where his father cemented his NBA legacy — after reportedly agreeing to a one-year, $6.5 million deal with the Heat. The move brings the younger Hardaway to the franchise where Tim Hardaway Sr. became one of its most iconic players. But while Hardaway Jr. will wear a Heat uniform, he won’t wear the No. 10 jersey hanging above Pat Riley Court at Kaseya Center. That number belongs to his father, and it is staying retired.

item.title: Mavericks targeting Anfernee Simons
