item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks have emerged as a suitor for Anfernee Simons, league sources say. As @JakeLFischer reported yesterday, Golden State and Miami have continued to pursue the free agent scoring guard who finished last season with Chicago.

item.title: Bradley Beal, Anfernee Simons, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope on Miami's radar
item.description: A source said some of the bigger guards on Miami’s radar are 6-4 Bradley Beal, 6-3 Anfernee Simons and 6-5 Kentavious Caldwell Pope (who is expected to part ways with the Grizzlies). The 6-5 Zach LaVine could become an option if the Kings waive and stretch the final season (and $49 million) of his contract. And so could....

item.title: The Heat has opted not to pursue former Heat guard …
item.description: The Heat has opted not to pursue former Heat guard Gabe Vincent, at this point, and is instead focusing on adding a guard with more size, a source with direct knowledge said. The Heat ideally would love to add that type of player at the minimum but possibly could offer more than that.

item.title: Vincent would have welcomed a Heat return and has …
item.description: Vincent would have welcomed a Heat return and has support internally, including from Bam Adebayo. The front office appreciates his maturity, toughness, grit and intelligence, among other attributes, but isn’t looking for another 6-2 point guard at this point after guaranteeing next season’s $2.6 million salary for 6-2 Dru Smith.

item.title: Warriors, Heat and Nuggets among teams interested in Anfernee Simons before signing with Philadelphia
item.description: Michael Scotto: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

item.title: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers …
item.description: NBA Base: Shams: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers already from contending teams, teams with aspirations… right now, the three that I mentioned — Golden State, Cleveland, Miami — those are seen as some of the types of teams that make logical sense for LeBron James.”

item.title: “The Heat culture, I feel like, is the biggest thing. …
item.description: “The Heat culture, I feel like, is the biggest thing. And I like to win, and they do a lot of that,” Donaldson said Thursday on a Zoom call with reporters when asked why he decided to sign with the Heat minutes after last month’s draft came to an end. “So just trying to be a part of that, trying to develop, understanding what I need to do for myself and my future. And just trying to get better. They do that really well and that’s what I was looking forward to.”

item.title: Giannis was about to sign off on the Celtics trade, …
item.description: Per Bill Simmons, Giannis was about to sign off on the Celtics trade, but he wanted a 3 year extension at 35% of the cap but Boston was set on 2 years at 30%. Brad Stevens would not budge on the extension and that’s when he approved the trade to Miami.

item.title: Miami would welcome LeBron James return
item.description: LeBron James left the Heat as a free agent in 2014 to return to Cavaliers, the team he left to join the Heat in 2010. The relationship between the Heat and James initially was frosty after their breakup, but is now strong. The Heat would welcome a return by James, a source has said.

item.title: If LeBron James’ interest in returning to the Heat is …
item.description: If LeBron James’ interest in returning to the Heat is serious, the team would have interest in making that happen, a league source conveyed earlier this week.

item.title: Guy like Giannis on his team, Wiggins, Off Night same …
item.description: Heat Central: Rich Paul and Max Kellerman on the positives about LeBron James going to Miami: Rich: You go down south. Mitchell, Wiggins, Giannis, Bam. Plus Pat, Plus Spo. Max: At LeBron’s age he can play defense here and there… At 42 he don’t have the energy to carry the offensive load and play defense on Every possession like 2012. What he needs is a guy like Bam on his team. Guy like Giannis on his team, Wiggins, Off Night same thing. Defensively it’s perfect for him. LeBron can shoot from hi

item.title: In a Zoom session with reporters on Friday morning, …
item.description: In a Zoom session with reporters on Friday morning, Heat forward Simone Fontecchio said he’s excited about playing with Giannis Antetokounmpo and said it was an easy decision to return to Miami, even on a minimum deal that will pay him $2.6 million next season. Fontecchio, who became the second person in the organization to speak extensively about the not-yet-announced Antetokounmpo trade, said the two-time MVP “can make life for other people around me so much easier. I know people who play with

item.title: “I always wanted to stay in Miami. Last week, they …
item.description: Speaking of his own decision to re-sign, he said: “I always wanted to stay in Miami. Last week, they said, ‘Listen we don’t have much room to operate.’ [But] my thought process was I want to stay here. I want to put some continuity to what I was doing here. There is a real big opportunity for the Heat with the Giannis trade and what it’s going to mean for the franchise and city.”

item.title: “I wanted to talk to him directly,” Fontecchio said. …
item.description: He said he spoke to Spoelstra before re-signing at the minimum. “I wanted to talk to him directly,” Fontecchio said. “We had a great conversation. I wanted to ask him a couple things. It wasn’t like he had to convince me. I was pretty much convinced. Hearing his voice, hearing his thought process and wanting me to stay helped me.” He said he “didn’t want to ask” about whether he would be a rotation player: “I’m not the type of guy that asks for reassurance. It’s dumb for me to ask for a guarante

item.title: The Miami Heat announced today that they have signed …
item.description: The Miami Heat announced today that they have signed guard Tre Donaldson to a Two-Way Contract

item.title: Heat's Pelle Larsson today with 19 points in Sweden's …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat's Pelle Larsson today with 19 points in Sweden's 87-76 victory today over Czechia in World Cup qualifying. Larsson closed 4 of 16 from the field, including 1 of 9 on 3-pointers. He went 10 of 11 from the line.

item.title: The two-way contract signed today with the Heat by …
item.description: Ira Winderman: The two-way contract signed today with the Heat by undrafted University of Miami guard Tre Donaldson is a one-year contract with a $90,000 G League guarantee, the largest guarantee allowed on such a deal. He is eligible at any time to be converted to a standard deal.

item.title: I give him two to three more years, and I want to save …
item.description: If I were to punch you on the spot with LeBron, who do you think he’s gonna end up signing with, and how many more years do you think he plays? Mario Chalmers: I give him two to three more years, and I want to save Miami, but I have a gut feeling that it might be Golden State.

item.title: "He's got a natural shooting ability and that's …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Heat summer league coach Wayne Ellington told us in the postgame briefing just now that Conwell's most NBA ready skill is his shooting: "He's got a natural shooting ability and that's something you can't teach." He said Conwell handled better than he expected. 21 points & several nice passes tonight, as he played both on and off the ball.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James deciding between Cavs, Heat, Nuggets, Sixers, Wolves, Warriors
item.description: Fullcourtpass: LeBron James will be making his free agent decision between the following six NBA teams, per @ShamsCharania - Cavaliers - Heat - Nuggets - Sixers - Timberwolves - Warriors

item.title: He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable …
item.description: James could experience a more organic farewell tour in his third stop in Cleveland, but he has fielded skepticism that he would join the Cavaliers on a veteran’s minimum deal. He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable option after they acquired Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks. The Heat could also offer a taxpayer mid-level exception between $11 or $12 million per years.

item.title: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources …
item.description: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources say, all still believe that they are in this race to some degree and getting legitimate consideration from James and his camp alongside the Cavaliers, Warriors and Heat.

item.title: Many teams still believe they have a chance at LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Sixers, the Timberwolves, the Heat, your Nuggets, based on everything I have been told all weekend, they all still believe they are in with the chance. I think the Warriors were portrayed as an early favorite, not the early favorite, an early favorite, because they were the team that we knew was determined to go after LeBron if he parted with the Lakers. We had known that for weeks leading up to LeBron and Rich Paul actually informing the Lakers, yes, we are definitely moving on

item.title: I just think that there are viable cases to be made …
item.description: Joe Vardon: "I told Rich that I thought this was the best or the most unique and that that had never been done before, what he did. It was absolutely incredible and unique, that entire message and the entire podcast, just to be so open about it. I think a lot of teams really are in the mix. I just think that there are viable cases to be made for Cleveland, Philadelphia, Miami, Golden State, and Dallas and the Timberwolves. I think those all those teams really are in play and I just feel like Ric

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is that that 2017-18 …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is that that 2017-18 Warriors team is a cheat code. I mean, they literally made the NBA say, you know what, forget vets, we're going to go all young guys on the bench. I mean, think about when that team came together. Only four teams thought they had a chance to really make a run: Warriors, Rockets, Cavs, and a coin flip of the East team was probably the Celtics because they were just prideful. Everyone else, they just started packing it in and saying like, we're j

item.title: This deep bond was on full display when Thanasis …
item.description: The Antetokounmpo brothers remain completely inseparable in every aspect of life, proving that family always comes first. This deep bond was on full display when Thanasis officially tied the knot with his beloved partner, Katia, during a beautiful ceremony in Sounio. Giannis did not merely attend to share in the joy of the occasion, but also proudly stepped up to serve as the best man for his sibling. The romantic wedding took place on Saturday, kicking off an elegant celebration that actively l

item.title: “Agreed to terms" becomes terms today at 12:01 p.m. in …
item.description: Ira Winderman: “Agreed to terms" becomes terms today at 12:01 p.m. in NBA at end of signing moratorium. Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis become Heat today, as does Tim Hardaway Jr., while Herro, Ware, Jakucionis, Jaquez and Powell become ex-Heat.

item.title: Giannis’ brother Alex Antetokounmpo on Davion …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis’ brother Alex Antetokounmpo on Davion Mitchell: “Amazing defender. Great playmaker as well. I think that he’s a really good player. They call him off night for a reason.”

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo on who is winning one-on-one matchup, him or Victor Wembanyama: Me, all day
item.description: Heat Central: Neon: Who you got in a 1v1 you or Wemby? Giannis: Me. All day. All day

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo on GOAT debate: The person I like to watch a lot is LeBron James
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo on who his GOAT is: The person I like to watch a lot is LeBron James. I like to watch him. But obviously Michael Jordan -- it's 1 and 2. Once it gets to that level, who cares? It's LeBron and MJ. Neon: LeBrons a Free Agent right now. Giannis: ayyyy

item.title: “I knew she would be my wife immediately…. She was …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo shares story of how he met his wife in Miami: “I knew she would be my wife immediately…. She was like ‘I want to get to know you’ so I said let’s meet in Miami. A lot of girls say ‘you gonna fly me out?’ (giannis shakes head). A week later I was in Miami, she showed up… When I saw her I said ‘that’s the person I can be with.’ From that moment she came to Miami until today, she never left my side.”

item.title: “Rivalries? Everybody. Bro I don’t f*ck with nobody.”
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo was asked if he has any rivalries in the NBA: “Rivalries? Everybody. Bro I don’t f*ck with nobody.”

item.title: Beasley: gotta handle some business first then we’ll …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis and his conversation with Malik Beasley 👀 Giannis: You coming back in the league? Beasley: gotta handle some business first then we’ll see Giannis: Give me a call, private Beasley: I’m in Miami too Giannis: You in Miami? I’ll be there in like a week. If you’re still there imma hit you

item.title: “I feel like when you work all summer to manipulate …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo says people who foul bait get rewarded but he gets hacked and doesn’t get the same calls: “I feel like when you work all summer to manipulate the game, you get rewarded for that. People that play straight forward like me, when I go down the lane and get hacked, and I say that’s a foul they tell me ‘you’re to strong. You have to show me that’s a foul.’ My job is not to show you it’s a foul my job is to play through contact. People that do the (flop) get calls a

item.title: Tim Hardaway Jr. will wear No. 10 in Miami. Tim …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: It should come as no surprise, but @TheJaxShow is correct. Tim Hardaway Jr. will wear No. 10 in Miami. Tim Hardaway Sr. changed his mind and will allow his son to wear his number with the Heat.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo deal between Miami, Milwaukee to remain a two-team trade
item.description: Anthony Chiang: It will be a two-team trade between the Heat and Bucks today, according to a league source. The NBA's moratorium has been lifted since 12:01 p.m.

item.title: So there is a chance ☝️
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis almost had a heart attack when Neon stream trolled him saying LeBron signed to Cleveland 👀 Giannis when he found out it’s fake: So there is a chance ☝️

item.title: Bam Adebayo. I’ll tell you right now it’s Bam Adebayo …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo says Bam Adebayo is the BEST American big man in the NBA: Alex: Who’s the best American big man Giannis: Bam Adebayo. I’ll tell you right now it’s Bam Adebayo bro

item.title: “Bam Adebayo, man that mf, he is good. BBQ chicken!”
item.description: Heat Central: GIANNIS ON BAM: “Bam Adebayo, man that mf, he is good. BBQ chicken!”

item.title: If LeBron chooses to sign elsewhere, one of those 2 …
item.description: Barry Jackson: If LeBron chooses to sign elsewhere, one of those 2 final roster spots (13th, 14th) could go to a big or perhaps a point guard. The other is expected to go to a wing, barring Heat change of heart... 15th spot could remain open to start the season.

item.title: Heat had no intention of adding draft assets to entice …
item.description: Marc Stein: As of yesterday, it was still a two-team trade with Bucks and Heat. (It's an unpredictable business, so always a chance something could crop up before finalized). But today's confirmation of signings isn't expected to include surprises. As @Anthony_Chiang and I said last Tuesday, Heat had no intention of adding draft assets to entice a team to take Nikola Jovic. (Miami has no more tradable firsts, anyway). Miami now waits on LeBron and also hopes to add another wing with size and len

item.title: Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized …
item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

item.title: Bucks owners release statement on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade as deal becomes official: Forever a Buck
item.description: Shams Charania: Statements from the Milwaukee Bucks on the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat: Bucks owners Wes Edens, Jimmy and Dee Haslam, and Jamie Dinan: “From delivering a championship to transforming our franchise, Giannis' impact on the Milwaukee Bucks is lasting and profound. We are incredibly grateful for what has been a remarkable and historic journey together. For 13 seasons, Giannis gave everything to this organization and inspired our city with his relentless drive, humil

item.title: This was an incredibly difficult decision, made for …
item.description: Shams Charania: Bucks general manager Jon Horst: “Since we drafted him in 2013, Giannis has transformed the Milwaukee Bucks in every way -- on the court, in our locker room and throughout the community. Over 13 seasons, he became an extraordinary leader, teammate and representative of this city, and one of the defining players of his generation. The standard he set will continue here. This was an incredibly difficult decision, made for what is best for the future of the Bucks and for Giannis. On

item.title: This transition also means saying goodbye to another …
item.description: Shams Charania: Jon Horst: This transition also means saying goodbye to another player who played an important role in one of the most successful eras in Bucks history. From the moment Bobby arrived, he embraced what it means to be a Buck and quickly became a fan favorite through his passion, competitiveness and unwavering commitment to winning. Bobby played a vital role in helping our franchise capture the 2021 NBA Championship, and his performance throughout that historic playoff run will fore

item.title: Giannis’ tribute to Milwaukee 💚
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Giannis’ tribute to Milwaukee 💚 (via @Giannis_An34 )

item.title: Pat Riley on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade: One of the great trades in Heat history, Giannis is a Top 5 player in the league
item.description: Your Miami HEAT announced today that they have acquired NBA Champion, Two-Time NBA MVP and 10-time NBA All-Star Giannis Antetokounmpo and NBA Champion Bobby Portis Jr. from the Milwaukee Bucks in exchange for Tyler Herro, Kasparas Jakučionis, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kel’el Ware, three first round picks (2026, 2031 & 2033), a 2030 first round pick swap and a 2033 second round pick. “The announcement of today’s trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis Jr. is one of the great trades in HEAT hist

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo says he might switch to No. 7 in Miami
item.description: Heat Central: BREAKING: Giannis Antetokounmpo announces he doesn’t think he will keep number 34 this year with the Heat. He likes the number 7. 😳

item.title: “I’m grateful for the Bucks. The fans. This is tough …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo live reaction after he was officially traded from the Bucks: “I’m grateful for the Bucks. The fans. This is tough moment for me right now but we’ll get through it. I’m grateful for the fans, memories, championships, my teammates, the city of Milwaukee. The state of Wisconsin”

item.title: The Giannis welcoming moment in Miami is expected to …
item.description: Ira Winderman: The Giannis welcoming moment in Miami is expected to be July 16, based on his travel schedule.

item.title: Will wear No. 95 for the #Heat. Number never worn in …
item.description: Etienne Catalan: Bobby Portis Jr. ( @BPortistime ) will wear No. 95 for the #Heat. Number never worn in franchise history. #NBA

item.title: Now that the Giannis trade has become official, below …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Now that the Giannis trade has become official, below are the extension options: As of 10/1 *⃣If 27-28 option declined: 3 years/$193M *⃣if 27-28 option exercised: 2 years/$135M As of 1/6 *⃣If 27-28 option declined: 4 years/$275M *⃣If 27-28 option exercised: 3 years/$214M

item.title: The difference in years and salary is a result of the …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The difference in years and salary is a result of the six-month extension restriction for players traded. The opt-in scenario (last one), maximizes the most earning power (an extra $1M).

item.title: From Tyler Herro, "To Spo, Pat, Micky, the entire …
item.description: Ira Winderman: From Tyler Herro, "To Spo, Pat, Micky, the entire organization, my teammates, the coaches, trainers, equipment staff, arena staff, and everyone who poured into me over the last seven years—thank you. Thank you for believing in me, pushing me, challenging me, and helping shape the player and person I’ve become."

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in Miami?
item.description: Barry Jackson: There has been no contact yet between DeRozan camp and Heat, per source just now. Miami would be among teams of interest to DeRozan, who is being set free by the Kings. Miami's options now include - among others - DeRozan, Beal, Gary Trent. Or the Heat simply could wait on LeBron decision before deciding what wing player to pursue. I expect Heat to try to add another wing; tough call is whether to pounce on one of these players or wait to see if a surprise name shakes free. Mavs -

item.title: today's Heat stats from World Cup qualifying:
item.description: Ira Winderman: today's Heat stats from World Cup qualifying: Nikola Jovic in Serbia 94-78 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina: 32 points on 11 of 16 FG, 7 of 9 on 3s, 3 blocks. Pelle Larsson in Sweden 88-79 win over Slovenia: 31 points on 10 of 21 FG, 1 of 8 on 3s, 10-15 FTs, 3 assists.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo will wear No. 7 for Miami
item.description: Tim Reynolds: It indeed appears Giannis will wear 7 in Miami. I wonder if the Heat should just keep selling 34 with a “+” in the middle.

item.title: Veterans minimum signings such as DeMar DeRozan, …
item.description: Heat Central: Veterans minimum signings such as DeMar DeRozan, Bradley Beal, and Khris Middleton could be on hold for Miami until LeBron makes his decision per, @JakeLFischer “We’re looking at Toronto, potentially Miami. All these veterans who could go to Miami and join Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo, they I think will all be on hold with LeBron’s situation” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: "Andrew Wiggins has proven to us that he is the …
item.description: Ira Winderman: The Heat have announced that Andrew Wiggins' contract extension is now official. Pat Riley, "Andrew Wiggins has proven to us that he is the quintessential small forward who fits perfectly with this roster. We feel he will be a critical part of what we’re trying to achieve with this team going forward and we feel fortunate that we were able to extend him."

item.title: "To Heat Nation, I just want to say thank you for …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Jaime Jaquez Jr. posted this message on his IG today: "To Heat Nation, I just want to say thank you for embracing and supporting me over the past 3 years. The city of Miami will always hold a special place in my heart. Thank you to the coaches and staff as well as the front office for giving me an opportunity to live out my dreams of playing in the NBA. It will always be love Miami."

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo leaves door open for Bucks' return
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo is open to returning to the Bucks later in his career "That would be awesome if I can go and play and maybe come back. Kevin Garnett did the same thing. I saw this clip of him walking into the [Timberwolves'] arena and [the fans] gave him a standing ovation and people to this day love him, and some people don't always feel—" (h/t @BucksLead)

item.title: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter …
item.description: Matt George: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter about in terms of potential landing destinations for DeMar DeRozan, the Miami Heat, who of course just traded for Giannis, and the Cleveland Cavaliers, who are a team I long thought could use the services of DeMar, especially in that second unit off of the bench. Of course, we've always heard LA ties, right? He is from LA, from Compton, very much a Los Angeles guy. So, we've always talked about the possibility of DeMar going home.

item.title: Warriors expected to check in on DeMar DeRozan
item.description: Anthony Slater: You know I see the Miami Heat. I anticipate them being interested potentially in DeMar DeRozan. The Warriors have already been told it's a guy they will look into at some point in the coming days, especially if they do not get LeBron James. There's some heartwarming potential landing spots right. You know the Clippers play in his hometown the Raptors. He's so famous. Those are two teams that compete that could use him. And the reality is he should be coming at such a bargain that

item.title: Tim Jr. is one of the most explosive shooters in the …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Pat Riley: “Hardaway is obviously a great name around here. Tim Jr. is one of the most explosive shooters in the league and a player we have long coveted. We are thrilled to finally be able to bring him home.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell would embrace Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James
item.description: Donovan Mitchell also would embrace the Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James if James makes the decision to sign with Cleveland, according to sources. The Cavaliers are among several leading suitors for James, joining Miami, Golden State, Philadelphia and Minnesota.

item.title: “All Giannis jersey orders and personalized …
item.description: With the Heat immediately starting to sell Giannis Antetokounmpo’s No. 34 jersey after his acquisition was finalized when the NBA’s moratorium lifted at 12:01 p.m. on Monday, that left the Heat searching for a remedy for those who purchased Antetokounmpo’s No. 34 Heat jersey before the number change became public a few hours later. “All Giannis jersey orders and personalized “ANTETOKOUNMPO” No. 34 jerseys will be fulfilled with the new No. 7,” according to a Heat spokesperson. “If fans purchased

item.title: Conwell didn’t have much input on the matter, saying …
item.description: Rookie guard Ryan Conwell was initially set to wear No. 7 with the Heat after being acquired by Miami in the second round of this year’s draft. But with Antetokounmpo now wearing to No. 7, Conwell is switching to No. 4. Conwell didn’t have much input on the matter, saying that somebody on the Heat’s equipment staff called him to say, “Yeah, big dog wants your number. You’re going to have to change.” “I mean, Giannis, he gets whatever he wants. I’m just now coming into the league,” Conwell said a

item.title: To recap: On Instagram last week, Herro posted a …
item.description: His off-court activity in the final days before he was shipped to Milwaukee included a classy and thoughtful farewell message on Monday but also included perplexing behavior that triggered a social media backlash days earlier. To recap: On Instagram last week, Herro posted a graphic listing the league’s top worst midrange shooters last season. And there, plain for everyone to see, was Giannis Antetokounmpo listed as the worst (at 26.8%) and Bam Adebayo listed as the 10th worst, at 35.9%. Then an

item.title: Adebayo, incidentally, has never been paid $60 …
item.description: We won’t list the quotes from that private spring conversation because they were shared publicly without his authorization. After the fan seemed to try to incite Herro and mentioned Bam Adebayo favorably, there was a comment — attributed to Herro — wondering whether a top tier defender on “some nights” should get paid $60 million. Adebayo, incidentally, has never been paid $60 million; his current extension tops out with a $57.4 million player option in 2028-29. Herro likely doesn’t want to call

item.description: Heat Central: Marc Stein on the interested LeBron teams and their free agent spending: “The Heat, Warriors, Cavaliers, 76ers, Timberwolves, and Nuggets, all of these teams are kind of watching every dollar right now to try and maintain absolute maximum flexibility in the event that LeBron says ‘yes, I’d like to come there.’”

item.title: He keeps a very tight circle and when it comes to …

Dwyane Wade and Bosh got about 12 hour heads up when he came to Miami.. he keeps a very tight circle and when it comes to announcing this FA he does a very good job of keeping a secret. item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: One of my biggest motivators in my career, one my favorite teammates also, Jabari Parker. Now I was able to kind of measure myself against him, right? If he works two hours, I work two hours and a half every single day. That was one of the guys that until today, I've never told him t

item.title: He was my favorite player growing up, I looked up to …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: In sports, our success is just winning the championship. Now there's some people that have never won a championship, like Charles Barkley, a lot of other players not just Charles Barkley but they have impacted the game, they’re part of the culture. Allen Iverson never won championships. He was my favorite player growing up, I looked up to him. I wanted to be him. I had cornrows because of him as a kid. So, and I was able to get older, work hard, make the NBA, and then win

item.title: Besides waiting for LeBron James, the Heat continues …
item.description: Besides waiting for LeBron James, the Heat continues to wait patiently to see if another shooter or scorer shakes free. They had initial conversations with Bradley Beal but haven’t been aggressive in their pursuit, per a source. They hadn’t contacted Demar DeRozan as of Monday, though that could change.

item.description: While DeRozan is interested in reuniting with the Raptors, NBA insider Jake Fischer revealed that the former NBA star might likely join teams that will fail to land James in this year's free agency. “He’s almost certainly going to be on a vet minimum somewhere else. Could it be Toronto? ... DeMar has interest in going back to the Raptors, but I think DeMar’s most likely landing spots are going to be teams that don’t get LeBron James," Fischer revealed on Bleacher Report. “I think the Miamis, Cle

item.description: Shams Charania: "I think there's a draw with Miami, and I think Pat Riley is a draw, I think Erik Spoelstra is a draw, once again. I haven't gotten the feel that there's any tension that still remains between LeBron James and Pat Riley that would deter him from that. I actually think Bam Adebayo and Giannis are big draws for him too. Like he played Team USA with Bam, he's got a lot of respect for Bam. item.title: "My goal is to win another championship. My goal is …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: "My goal is to win another championship. My goal is for my kids to be healthy and happy."

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo: 'I want to be a coach in the NBA'
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo on his post-retirement plans: "Either I'm going to be a stay-at-home dad, or I'm going to be a coach. I want to be a coach in the NBA."

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo: "I think for some few years, …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: "I think for some few years, I'll go ghost because I want to be able to do my own thing. We play every other days, we don't have time for ourselves. So I want to be in my kids' tournaments and their plays at school, just being in their life a lot more."

Basically if he chooses somebody besides CLE or MIA. CLE or MIA, it’s a very clean decision. It makes sense. Both of them have cases. Does he want to deal with in his life what the reaction will be if he we

item.description: Heat Central: “I think Giannis is clearly hopeful that LeBron will come and I think that was a good sign… If you’re LeBron you think it’s cool that Giannis is so wrapped up in what you might do and certainly the Heat are still absolutely on the board.” — @WindhorstESPN on LeBron to Miami rumors

item.title: Bucks GM Jon Horst: 'No one's sitting here today and saying that we're a better team today after trading Giannis'
item.description: Milwaukee Bucks general manager Jon Horst called the move to trade Giannis Antetokounmpo a "very difficult decision," but he also acknowledged that it was a transition that was in the best interest of both the Bucks and Antetokounmpo. "No one's sitting here today and saying that we're a better team today after trading Giannis," Horst said during a conference call with reporters Wednesday. "He's one of the greatest players to ever play, greatest player in the franchise history. We've had an incre

item.title: "This was not a 'have to,'" Horst said. "This was, we …
item.description: In the offseason, the Bucks replaced coach Doc Rivers with Taylor Jenkins. Yet Horst denied feeling any pressure that the team was forced to trade Antetokounmpo this summer. "This was not a 'have to,'" Horst said. "This was, we truly believed we've found an opportunity which is unique. We might be right. We might be wrong. But an opportunity where this is what's best for him and what he wants to pursue going forward, and this is what's best for us and what we want to pursue going forward. And th

Despite his own team's reported interest, Schefter believes the four-time MVP is headed to South Florida. “I still think that he's going to wind up in Miami. In Miami.”

I’m honestly trying to work with you and build some chemistry,’” Mitchell recalled from his conversation with Antetokounmpo that led to the plan to spend time together in Greece later this month. “He was like, ‘Do you ever go to Europe?’ And I was like, ‘actually, I’m

item.title: “I think we have a defensive-minded team, which is …
item.description: Mitchell, who is a defensive-minded guard, envisions a Heat defense led by six-time NBA All-Defensive Team selection Bam Adebayo and a former NBA Defensive Player of the Year in Antetokounmpo that will be “really scary.” “I think we have a defensive-minded team, which is really good,” Mitchell, 27, said. “Because I think offense, we’ll learn. item.title: So, I think the percentages are going to look better …

Everyone’s going to get a lot because of how much [Antetokounmpo] brings to the court and how much gravity he brings. So, I think the percentages are going to look better because we’re going to get more wide open shots instead of, like, we’ve got to create our shot. Throughout the game, obviously, you get open shots. But with him, there’s going to be so many open shots that people don’t understand that. B

They play for championships. It's part of the business. I know how good I am and what I'm capable of. I just have to keep working, stay healthy, and represent this new team how i'm supposed too."

The incident took place at a gym inside the Resorts World Casino in front of Herro’s AAU team. According to multiple source who witnessed the incident and were granted anonymity to discuss a sensitive matter, Adebayo walked onto the courts and Herro said something to him. Adebayo proceed to walk over to Herro and, without hesitation,

So I wanted to change it up a little bit. I was looking up at the sign and it said ‘95 South.’ And if you don’t know, you can take 95 all the way to Miami. So 95 is where I’m at now.”

And obviously not knowing Milwaukee, but I kind of figured I was going to get traded this summer. I’m excited and happy it’s Milwaukee.”

item.title: “That’s part of it,” Herro said. “I think, I’m sure …
item.description: Herro was involved in plenty of trade speculation throughout his seven seasons with the Heat. Whether it was for Damian Lillard, Kevin Durant, James Harden or Antetokounmpo, Herro was essentially part of trade rumors during every offseason of his Heat tenure. “That’s part of it,” Herro said. “I think, I’m sure there will still be rumors in Miami somewhere for someone. I’ve said it though, Miami is an organization that wants to win championships and they want to compete. We’ve obviously almost go

Thanks also to the Arison Family and the HEAT organization who have made an imp

He is the modern day Udonis Haslem. They consider him the absolute franchise pillar. I’ve always heard nothing but he’s held in high regards by his teammates… He is the untouchable there. item.title: LeBron James to the Warriors still a possibility?

As much as the reunion with Cleveland and Miami would be storybook, this new all star team, traveling team USA kind of circus, high entertainment value thing that would exist is GS, is something that is still very much on the table.”

item.title: “I go in every situation wanting to be where I’m at …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Norman Powell on the flexible nature of his deal: “I go in every situation wanting to be where I’m at and help win. Looking at the business of it, I’ve been around so long that it crossed my mind. But I’m focused on being in the moment.” Said he wanted to return to Miami but is understanding of the apron’s effect on teams and wants to be where he’s valued.

item.title: Congratulations to Coach Bob McAdoo on being awarded …

item.title: Cavs, Heat and Sixers frontrunners in LeBron James sweepstakes?
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James: “Every team that’s in it still believes they have a chance and is still engaged with Rich Paul on a daily basis, hourly basis. Figuring out what it would look like, what the vision is. CLE, MIA, GSW, MIN, PHI, this are the 5 most teams that I’ve been hearing…. When I talk to other teams, they look at Cleveland a lot, Miami, and Philadelphia. Those are the 3 teams that others view as strong here.” (Via @SiriusXMNBA)

item.title: Dwyane Wade says it’s difficult for LeBron James to …
item.description: The HEAT Realm: Dwyane Wade says it’s difficult for LeBron James to retire because he’s still playing at an elite level: “I’m just here to appreciate what I’m witnessing…because we’ve never seen this before. It has to suck a little bit to get to this point in your career and still be really good….like I can still play — I can go play right now and still be able to play but I won’t be that good so it’s easy for me to decide to walk away. It’s hard to make that decision when you’re still really f*

item.title: “There were some leaked direct messages on IG I …
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on the Bam Adebayo and Tyler Herro altercation: “There were some leaked direct messages on IG I believe where Tyler Herro was critiquing Bam Adebayo and his viability as a $60M player. Bam approached Tyler Herro about this. They had a back and forth that ended with them getting into it physically where Bam Adebayo struck Tyler Herro… There were other players around, multiple teams that used this practice court. This was in a pretty public domain.” Shams also later said he’s n

item.title: Kel'el Ware addresses trade to Bucks
item.description: This year, Kel'el Ware is walking around Las Vegas during summer league in Milwaukee Bucks gear after the Heat included him as part of a trade package to acquire two-time NBA MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo and veteran forward Bobby Portis this offseason. “I mean, I kind of figured [the trade] was going to happen,” Ware said to two South Florida newspapers, including the Miami Herald, on Friday while watching the Bucks and Heat face off in a Las Vegas Summer League game. “So I was just preparing mysel

item.title: “That helps out a lot more,” Ware said of being traded …
item.description: Now, Ware can continue his growth alongside three former Heat teammates in Milwaukee. The Heat traded guard Tyler Herro, forward Jaime Jaquez Jr. and guard Kasparas Jakucionis along with Ware to the Bucks. “That helps out a lot more,” Ware said of being traded to the Bucks with Herro, Jaquez and Jakucionis. “Some guys, they get traded by themselves. So I would say it helps to have three guys with me.”
